text.title: HealthTech FHIR BA Pack Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every HealthTech FHIR BA Pack application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Map every patient-data field to an FHIR resource (Patient, Encounter, Observation, Condition, Procedure, MedicationStatement). Custom fields require a FHIR extension.
rule.rationale: FHIR is the interop standard; ignoring it adds 2-5× integration cost at later stages.

rule.statement: Business Associate Agreement (HIPAA) or Data Processing Agreement (GDPR) signed BEFORE any patient data is touched. No exceptions.
rule.rationale: Unsigned BAA + touching PHI = statutory violation regardless of intent.

rule.statement: If the engagement is research, IRB protocol + informed consent flow are designed BEFORE software design. Consent revocation must be honored end-to-end.
rule.rationale: Research without IRB is unethical + uninsurable; consent revocation must propagate to all derived data.

rule.statement: Audit log of every PHI access is immutable (append-only, hash-chained, OR external WORM storage). Mutable logs fail HIPAA audit.
rule.rationale: Mutable logs are not legal evidence; immutability is the only safe design.

rule.statement: Apply minimum-necessary standard: every PHI access is gated to the least data required for the role. No 'select * from patients' anywhere.
rule.rationale: HIPAA minimum-necessary is enforced in audit; broad SELECTs are immediate findings.
